   543	  def assign_initial_attributes
   544	    self.short_id = ShortId.new(self.class).generate

   546	    self.hotness = calculated_hotness

   549	
   550	  def calculated_hotness
   551	    # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
   552	    # stories submitted by the author
   553	    base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)
   554	
   555	    # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
   556	    cpoints = if base < 0
   557	      0
   558	    else
   559	      merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
   560	    end
   561	
   562	    # mix in any stories this one cannibalized
   563	    cpoints += merged_stories.map(&:score).inject(&:+).to_f
   564	
   565	    # if a story has many comments but few votes, it's probably a bad story, so
   566	    # cap the comment points at the number of upvotes
   567	    cpoints = [self.score, cpoints].min
   568	
   569	    # don't immediately kill stories at 0 by bumping up score by one
   570	    order = Math.log([(score + 1).abs + cpoints, 1].max, 10)
   571	    sign = if score > 0
   572	      1
   573	    elsif score < 0
   574	      -1
   575	    else
   576	      0
   577	    end
   578	
   579	    -((order * sign) + base + ((created_at || Time.current).to_f / HOTNESS_WINDOW)).round(7)
   580	  end

content.text: 1 # typed: false 2 3 class Comment < ApplicationRecord 4 belongs_to :user 5 belongs_to :story, 6 inverse_of: :comments, 7 touch: true 8 # has_one :comment_stat, -> { where("date(created_at)", } 9 has_many :votes, 10 dependent: :delete_all 11 belongs_to :parent_comment, 12 class_name: "Comment", 13 inverse_of: false, 14 optional: true, 15 counter_cache: :reply_count, 16 touch: true 17 has_one :moderation, 18 class_name: "Moderation", 19 inverse_of: :comment, 20 dependent: :restrict_with_exception 21 belongs_to :hat, 22 optional: true 23 has_many :taggings, through: :story 24 has_many :links, inverse_of: :from_comment, dependent: :restrict_with_exception 25 has_many :incoming_links, 26 class_name: "Link", 27 inverse_of: :to_comment, 28 dependent: :restrict_with_exception 29 has_many :notifications, as: :notifiable 30 31 include Token 32 33 attr_accessor :current_vote, :current_reply, :previewing, :vote_summary 34 35 validates :short_id, presence: true, uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false} 36 37 before_validation :assign_initial_attributes, on: :create 38 39 # We log hats before the transaction ends so we can accurately check whether the model changed 40 after_save :log_hat_use 41 # Calling :record_initial_upvote from after_commit instead of after_create minimizes how many 42 # queries happen inside the transaction, which seems to be the cause of bug #1238. 43 after_commit :mark_submitter, :record_initial_upvote, on: :create 44 after_commit :recreate_links, :update_associated_caches 45 46 scope :deleted, -> { where(is_deleted: true) } 47 scope :not_deleted, -> { where(is_deleted: false) } 48 scope :not_moderated, -> { where(is_moderated: false) } 49 scope :active, -> { not_deleted.not_moderated } 50 # Only use accessible_to_user in views that render comments in the flat style. The first two bytes map confidence to a big-endian unsigned integer, inverted so 347 # that high-confidence have low values. confidence is based on the number of upvotes and flags, 348 # so some values (like the one for 1 vote, 0 flags) are very common, causing sibling comments to 349 # tie. The large model read hit output truncation, so I’ll switch to narrower file reads and search slices. The important pattern so far: `Comment`, `Vote`, `HiddenStory`, `SavedStory`, `ReadRibbon`, suggestions, links, moderation, and user stats all encode direct story assumptions.

   747	    return false if user.nil? || user.new_record? || # assumption: cabinet view
   748	      new_record? # assumption: previewing
   749	
   750	    if user&.id == user_id
   751	      if is_moderated?
   752	        false
   753	      else
   754	        created_at.after?(MAX_EDIT_MINS.minutes.ago)
   755	      end
   756	    else
   757	      false
   758	    end
   759	  end
   760	
   761	  def is_gone?
   762	    is_deleted? || (user.is_banned? && score < 0)
   763	  end
   764	
   765	  def is_hidden_by_user?(user)
   766	    !!HiddenStory.find_by(user_id: user.id, story_id: id)
   767	  end
   768	
   769	  def is_recent?
   770	    created_at >= RECENT_DAYS.days.ago
   771	  end
   772	
   773	  def is_saved_by_user?(user)
   774	    !!SavedStory.find_by(user_id: user.id, story_id: id)
   775	  end
   776	
   777	  def is_unavailable
   778	    !unavailable_at.nil?
   779	  end
   780	
   781	  def is_unavailable=(what)
   782	    self.unavailable_at = ((what.to_i == 1 && !is_unavailable) ? Time.current : nil)
   783	  end
   784	
   785	  def is_undeletable_by_user?(user)
   786	    if user&.is_moderator?
   787	      true
   788	    elsif user && user.id == user_id && !is_moderated?
   789	      true
   790	    else
   791	      false
   792	    end
   793	  end
   794	
   795	  def log_moderation
   796	    if new_record? || (!editing_from_suggestions && (!editor || editor.id == user_id))
   797	      return
   798	    end
   799	
   800	    all_changes = changes.merge(tag_changes)
   801	    all_changes.delete("normalized_url")
   802	    all_changes.delete("unavailable_at")
   803	    all_changes.delete("last_edited_at")
   804	
   805	    if !all_changes.any?
   806	      return
   807	    end
   808	
   809	    m = Moderation.new
   810	    if editing_from_suggestions
   811	      m.is_from_suggestions = true
   812	    else
   813	      m.moderator_user_id = editor.try(:id)
   814	    end
   815	    m.story_id = id
   816	
   817	    m.action = all_changes.map { |k, v|
   818	      if k == "is_deleted" && is_deleted?
   819	        "deleted story"
   820	      elsif k == "is_deleted" && !is_deleted?
   821	        "undeleted story"
   822	      elsif k == "merged_story_id"
   823	        if v[1]
   824	          "merged into #{merged_into_story.short_id} " \
   825	            "(#{merged_into_story.title})"
   826	        else
   827	          "unmerged from another story"
   828	        end
   829	      elsif v[0].is_a?(String) && v[1].is_a?(String) && v[0].present? && v[1].blank?
   830	        "blanked #{k} from #{v[0].inspect}"
   831	      else
   832	        "changed #{k} from #{v[0].inspect} to #{v[1].inspect}"
   833	      end
   834	    }.join(", ")
   835	
   836	    m.reason = moderation_reason
   837	    m.save!
   838	
   839	    self.is_moderated = true
   840	  end
   841	
   842	  def mailing_list_message_id
   843	    "story.#{short_id}.#{created_at.to_i}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
   844	  end
   845	
   846	  def mark_submitter
   847	    Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{user_id}:stories_submitted")
   848	  end
   849	
   850	  # unordered, use Comment.thread_sorted_comments for presenting threads
   851	  def merged_comments
   852	    return Comment.none unless id # unsaved Stories have no comments
   853	
   854	    Comment.joins(:story)
   855	      .where(story: {merged_story_id: id})
   856	      .or(Comment.where(story_id: id))
   857	  end
   858	
   859	  def merge_story_short_id=(sid)
   860	    self.merged_story_id = sid.present? ? Story.where(short_id: sid).pick(:id) : nil
   861	  end
   862	
   863	  def merge_story_short_id
   864	    merged_story_id ? merged_into_story.try(:short_id) : nil
   865	  end
   866	
   867	  def record_initial_upvote
   868	    Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(1, id, nil, user_id, nil, false)
   869	  end
   870	
   871	  def show_score_to_user?(u)
   872	    u&.is_moderator? || !current_flagged?
   873	  end
   874	
   875	  def tag_changes
   876	    # 'tags_was' is bad grammar but is named to mirror ActiveModel::Dirty's convention of providing
   877	    # a 'attribute_was' reader. The AR associations API is pretty broad, so rather than try to
   878	    # override all the methods to maintain state, this exception should prompt you to only use
   879	    # the tags= method. See StoriesController#update for an example.
   880	    raise "Controller didn't save tags_was for edit logging" if tags_was.nil?
   881	
   882	    old_tag_names = tags_was.map(&:tag).join(" ")
   883	    new_tag_names = tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")
   884	
   885	    if old_tag_names == new_tag_names
   886	      {}
   887	    else
   888	      {"tags" => [old_tag_names, new_tag_names]}
   889	    end
   890	  end
   891	
   892	  def save_suggested_tags_for_user!(new_tag_names_a, user)
   893	    suggested_taggings.where(user_id: user.id).delete_all
   894	
   895	    new_suggested_tags = Tag
   896	      .active
   897	      .where(tag: new_tag_names_a.uniq.compact_blank)
   898	      .map { |t| {user: user, story: self, tag: t} }
   899	    SuggestedTagging.create!(new_suggested_tags)
   900	
   901	    # if enough users voted on the same set of replacement tags, do it
   902	    tag_votes = {}
   903	    suggested_taggings.group_by(&:user_id).each do |_u, stg|
   904	      stg.each do |s|
   905	        tag_votes[s.tag.tag] ||= 0
   906	        tag_votes[s.tag.tag] += 1
   907	      end
   908	    end
   909	
   910	    final_tags = []
   911	    tag_votes.each do |k, v|
   912	      if v >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
   913	        final_tags.push k
   914	      end
   915	    end
   916	
   917	    if final_tags.any? && (final_tags.sort != tags.map(&:tag).sort)
   918	      # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested tags " \
   919	      #  "#{final_tags.inspect} instead of #{tags.map(&:tag).inspect}"
   920	      self.editor = nil
   921	      self.editing_from_suggestions = true
   922	      self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
   923	      self.tags_was = tags.to_a
   924	      Story.transaction do
   925	        self.tags = Tag.where(tag: final_tags)
   926	        save!
   927	      end
   928	    end
   929	  end
   930	
   931	  def save_suggested_title_for_user!(title, user)
   932	    st = suggested_titles.find_by(user_id: user.id)
   933	    if !st
   934	      st = suggested_titles.build
   935	      st.user_id = user.id
   936	    end
   937	    st.title = title
   938	    st.save!
   939	
   940	    # if enough users voted on the same exact title, save it
   941	    title_votes = {}
   942	    suggested_titles.each do |s|
   943	      title_votes[s.title] ||= 0
   944	      title_votes[s.title] += 1
   945	    end
   946	
   947	    title_votes.sort_by { |_k, v| v }.reverse_each do |kv|
   948	      if kv[1] >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
   949	        # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested title " \
   950	        #   "#{kv[0].inspect} instead of #{self.title.inspect}"
   951	        self.editor = nil
   952	        self.editing_from_suggestions = true
   953	        self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
   954	        self.title = kv[0]
   955	        self.tags_was = tags.to_a
   956	        if !save
   957	          # Rails.logger.error "[s#{id}] failed auto promoting: " << errors.inspect
   958	        end
   959	
   960	        break
   961	      end
   962	    end
   963	  end
   964	
   965	  INVISIBLE_CHARACTERS = Regexp.union(
   966	    # Sourced from https://regex101.com/r/42jDca/1
   967	    /\u0000-\u001F/, # control characters
   968	    /\uFFF0-\uFFF8/, # interlinear annotation
   969	    /\u007F/, # DELETE
   970	    # and from https://invisible-characters.com/
   971	    /\u3164/, # HANGUL FILLER
   972	    /\uFFA0/, # HALFWIDTH HANGUL FILLER
   973	    /\uFFFC/, # OBJECT REPLACEMENT CHARACTER
   974	    /\u00A0/, # NO-BREAK SPACE
   975	    /\u00AD/, # SOFT HYPHEN
   976	    /\u034F/, # COMBINING GRAPHEME JOINER
   977	    /\u061C/, # ARABIC LETTER MARK
   978	    /\u115F/, # HANGUL CHOSEONG FILLER
   979	    /\u1160/, # HANGUL JUNGSEONG FILLER
   980	    /\u1680/, # OGHAM SPACE MARK

### app/models/domain.rb
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class Domain < ApplicationRecord
     4	  has_many :stories
     5	  belongs_to :banned_by_user,
     6	    class_name: "User",
     7	    inverse_of: false,
     8	    optional: true
     9	  has_many :origins, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
    10	  has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
    11	
    12	  include Token
    13	
    14	  validates :banned_reason, length: {maximum: 200}
    15	  validates :domain, presence: true, length: {maximum: 255}, uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false}
    16	  validates :selector, length: {maximum: 255}
    17	  validates :replacement, length: {maximum: 255}
    18	  validates :stories_count, numericality: {only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0}, presence: true
    19	
    20	  validate :valid_selector
    21	
    22	  after_save :update_origins
    23	
    24	  def valid_selector
    25	    return if selector.nil?
    26	
    27	    if selector.include? "\n"
    28	      errors.add(:selector, "no newlines")
    29	      return
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    selector_regexp
    33	  rescue RegexpError => e
    34	    errors.add(:selector, "is an invalid Regexp: #{e.message}")
    35	  end
    36	
    37	  def self./(domain)
    38	    find_by! domain:
    39	  end
    40	
    41	  def selector=(s)
    42	    s = s.strip
    43	    s = "\\A#{s}" unless s.starts_with?("\\A")
    44	    s = "#{s}\\z" unless s.ends_with?("\\z")
    45	    super
    46	  end
    47	
    48	  def selector_regexp
    49	    Regexp.new(selector, Regexp::IGNORECASE, timeout: 0.1)
    50	  end
    51	
    52	  def update_origins
    53	    return unless saved_change_to_selector? || saved_change_to_replacement?
    54	
    55	    stories.find_each do |story|
    56	      story.update_column(:origin_id, story.domain.find_or_create_origin(story.url)&.id)
    57	    end
    58	  end
    59	
    60	  def find_or_create_origin(url)
    61	    return nil if selector.blank? || replacement.blank?
    62	    valid?
    63	    raise ArgumentError, "Domain not valid: #{errors.full_messages.join(", ")}" if errors.any?
    64	    raise ArgumentError, "Can't create Origin until Domain is persisted" if new_record?
    65	
    66	    # github.com/foo -> github.com/foo
    67	    # github.com/foo/bar -> github.com/foo
    68	    identifier = if url.match?(selector_regexp)
    69	      url.sub(selector_regexp, replacement)
    70	    else
    71	      # if the URL isn't matched, the identifier is the bare domain (handles root + partial regexps)
    72	      domain
    73	    end.downcase
    74	
    75	    # because of rails associations, `origins` is scoped to current domain object
    76	    # create_or_find_by returns the new origin record
    77	    origins.find_or_create_by(identifier: identifier)
    78	  end
    79	
    80	  def ban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
    81	    self.banned_at = Time.current
    82	    self.banned_by_user_id = banner.id
    83	    self.banned_reason = reason
    84	    save!
    85	
    86	    m = Moderation.new
    87	    m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
    88	    m.domain = self
    89	    m.action = "Banned"
    90	    m.reason = reason
    91	    m.save!
    92	  end
    93	
    94	  def unban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
    95	    self.banned_at = nil
    96	    self.banned_by_user_id = nil
    97	    self.banned_reason = nil
    98	    save!
    99	
   100	    m = Moderation.new
   101	    m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
   102	    m.domain = self
   103	    m.action = "Unbanned"
   104	    m.reason = reason
   105	    m.save!
   106	  end
   107	
   108	  def banned?
   109	    banned_at?
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  def n_submitters
   113	    stories.count("distinct user_id")
   114	  end
   115	
   116	  def to_param
   117	    domain
   118	  end
   119	end

    31	  scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }

    33	  scope :related, ->(tag) {
    34	    active
    35	      .joins(:taggings)
    36	      .where(taggings: {story_id: Tagging.where(tag: tag).select(:story_id)})
    37	      .where.not(id: [tag, 67]) # 67 = programming, the catch-all
    38	      .where.not(is_media: true)

    51	    Tag.active.order(:tag).select { |t| t.can_be_applied_by?(user) }.map { |t|
    52	      t.filtered_count = counts[t.id].to_i
    53	      t

    93	      end

    95	      m.tag_id = id

    97	  end
    98

    18	
    19	  def exists?
    20	    path.exist?
    21	  end
    22	
    23	  def generate(url)
    24	    return if url.blank?
    25	
    26	    card_image_url = find_card_image_url(url)
    27	    return if card_image_url.blank?
    28	
    29	    story_card_image = generated_card_image(card_image_url)
    30	
    31	    if story_card_image.present?
    32	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(CACHE_DIR)
    33	
    34	      temp_filename = ".#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png"
    35	      temp_path = CACHE_DIR.join(temp_filename)
    36	
    37	      File.open(temp_path, "wb+") do |f|
    38	        f.write story_card_image
    39	      end
    40	
    41	      File.rename(temp_path, path)
    42	    end
    43	  end
    44	
    45	  private
    46	
    47	  def find_card_image_url(url)
    48	    response = fetch_url(url)
    49	    return nil unless response
    50	
    51	    # If it's too large, skip
    52	    return nil if response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
    53	
    54	    # Clean the content-type
    55	    content_type = response["content-type"].to_s.downcase.split(";").first.strip
    56	
    57	    # Check for a direct image url
    58	    return url if VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(content_type)
    59	
    60	    return nil unless content_type == "text/html"
    61	
    62	    converted = response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
    63	    parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
    64	
    65	    # Try <meta property="og:image">
    66	    card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
    67	
    68	    # Then try <meta name="image">
    69	    if card_image_url.blank?
    70	      card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[name='image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    card_image_url
    74	  rescue => e
    75	    Rails.logger.error "Error finding image URL for story: #{e.message}"
    76	    nil
    77	  end
    78	
    79	  def generated_card_image(url)
    80	    res = fetch_url(url)
    81	    return nil if res.blank?
    82	
    83	    # Limit the image size according to Meta developer docs
    84	    # https://developers.facebook.com/docs/sharing/webmasters/images/
    85	    return nil if res.body.length >= 8.megabytes
    86	
    87	    add_logo(res.body)
    88	  rescue => e
    89	    Rails.logger.error "Error generating story image: #{e.message}"
    90	    nil
    91	  end
    92	
    93	  def add_logo(image_data)
    94	    lobsters_logo = Vips::Image.new_from_file(Rails.public_path.join("touch-icon-144.png").to_s)
    95	    image = Vips::Image.new_from_buffer(image_data, "")
    96	
    97	    # since the lobsters icon image is a RGB, we need to ensure the image is RGB as well.
    98	    image = image.flatten(background: [255, 255, 255]) if image.bands > 3
    99	
   100	    # Resize lobsters_logo if it's too big compared to the image
   101	    if lobsters_logo.height > image.height / 5
   102	      lobsters_logo = lobsters_logo.resize((image.height / 5.0) / lobsters_logo.height)
   103	    end
   104	
   105	    # If it looks like the logo is mispositioned, it is likely due to social media
   106	    # cropping behaviors on preview cards.
   107	    #
   108	    # References:
   109	    # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/PreviewCard/
   110	    # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/MediaAttachment/#image
   111	    # https://mastodon.online/@mastodonmigration/109440513681868009
   112	    combined_image = image.insert(lobsters_logo, 0, image.height - lobsters_logo.height)
   113	    combined_image.write_to_buffer(".png")
   114	  rescue Vips::Error => e
   115	    Rails.logger.error "Vips error while generating image: #{e.message}"
   116	    nil
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def fetch_url(url)
   120	    s = Sponge.new
   121	    s.timeout = 3
   122	    headers = {
   123	      "Referer" => Routes.story_short_id_url(@short_id)
   124	    }
   125	    s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3)
   126	  end
   127	end

     9	
    10	  # for comment_vote_summaries
    11	  attribute :count, :integer
    12	  attribute :usernames, :string
    13	
    14	  validates :vote, presence: true, inclusion: {in: [1, -1]}
    15	  validates :reason,
    16	    length: {is: 1},
    17	    allow_blank: true,
    18	    presence: true
    19	  validate :validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
    20	
    21	  scope :comments_flags, ->(comments, user = nil) {

    25	
    26	  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
    27	  COMMENT_REASONS = {
    28	    "O" => "Off-topic",
    29	    "M" => "Me-too",
    30	    "T" => "Troll",
    31	    "U" => "Unkind",
    32	    "S" => "Spam",

    34	  }.freeze
    35	  ALL_COMMENT_REASONS = COMMENT_REASONS.merge({
    36	    "I" => "Incorrect"

    38	
    39	  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
    40	  STORY_REASONS = {
    41	    "O" => "Off-topic",

    67	  def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)
    68	    Vote
    69	      .joins(:user)
    70	      .select("comment_id, reason, count(1) as count, group_concat(username separator ', ') as usernames")
    71	      .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
    72	      .where.not(reason: "")
    73	      .group(:comment_id, :reason)
    74	      .group_by(&:comment_id)

    77	  def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)
    78	    votes = {}
    79	
    80	    Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,
    81	      comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
    82	      votes[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    votes

    87	
    88	  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
    89	    votes = {}
    90	
    91	    Vote.where(
    92	      user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id
    93	    ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
    94	      votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    95	    end

    97	    votes

    99	
   100	  def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
   101	    if story_ids.empty?
   102	      {}
   103	    else
   104	      votes = where(
   105	        user_id: user_id,
   106	        comment_id: nil,
   107	        story_id: story_ids
   108	      )
   109	      votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
   110	        memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
   111	      end
   112	    end
   113	  end
   114	
   115	  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
   116	    return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?
   117	
   118	    votes = where(
   119	      user_id: user_id,
   120	      comment_id: comment_ids
   121	    ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
   122	    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
   123	      memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
   124	    end
   125	  end
   126	
   127	  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
   128	    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
   129	  )
   130	    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
   131	      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize
   132	
   133	    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
   134	    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
   135	    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
   136	    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
   137	    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
   138	    # happen with associated comments.
   139	    v.story if story_id.present?
   140	
   141	    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change
   142	
   143	    # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
   144	    # no vote  -> 1      1
   145	    # flag -> 1          1
   146	    # no vote  -> flag   0
   147	    # flag -> no vote    0
   148	    # 1  -> no vote     -1
   149	    # 1  -> flag        -1
   150	    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
   151	      1
   152	    elsif v.vote == 1
   153	      -1
   154	    else
   155	      0
   156	    end
   157	    flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
   158	      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
   159	      -1
   160	    elsif new_vote == -1
   161	      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
   162	      1
   163	    else
   164	      # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
   165	      0
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    if new_vote == 0
   169	      v.destroy!
   170	    else
   171	      v.vote = new_vote
   172	      v.reason = reason
   173	      v.save!
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    if update_counters
   177	      t = v.target
   178	      if v.user_id != t.user_id
   179	        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta
   180	      end
   181	      t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
   182	    end
   183	  end
   184	
   185	  def target
   186	    if comment_id
   187	      Comment.find(comment_id)
   188	    else
   189	      Story.find(story_id)
   190	    end
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
   194	    if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
   195	      errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
   196	    end
   197	  end
   198	end

### app/models/link.rb
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class Link < ApplicationRecord
     4	  belongs_to :from_story, class_name: "Story", optional: true
     5	  belongs_to :from_comment, class_name: "Comment", optional: true
     6	  belongs_to :to_story, class_name: "Story", optional: true
     7	  belongs_to :to_comment, class_name: "Comment", optional: true
     8	
     9	  validates :url, length: {maximum: 250, allow_nil: false}, presence: true
    10	  validate :valid_url
    11	  validates :normalized_url, length: {maximum: 255, allow_nil: false}, presence: true
    12	  validates :title, length: {maximum: 255}
    13	  validate :validate_from_presence_xor
    14	  validate :validate_only_one_to
    15	
    16	  # rubocop:disable Rails/UniqueValidationWithoutIndex
    17	  # no database-level index because, per https://mariadb.com/kb/en/getting-started-with-indexes/
    18	  # > In SQL any NULL is never equal to anything, not even to another NULL. Consequently, a UNIQUE
    19	  # > constraint will not prevent one from storing duplicate rows if they contain null values:
    20	  validates :url, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}
    21	  validates :to_comment, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}, if: ->(l) { l.to_comment.present? }
    22	  validates :to_story, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}, if: ->(l) { l.to_story.present? }
    23	  # rubocop:enable Rails/UniqueValidationWithoutIndex
    24	
    25	  scope :recently_linked_from_comments, ->(url) {
    26	    joins(:from_comment).includes(:from_comment)
    27	      .where(from_comment: {created_at: (7.days.ago)..})
    28	      .where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(url))
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  def url=(u)
    32	    return if u.blank?
    33	
    34	    super(u.to_s.strip)
    35	    self.normalized_url = Utils.normalize(url)
    36	
    37	    if normalized_url.starts_with? Rails.application.domain
    38	      path = normalized_url.delete_prefix(Rails.application.domain)
    39	      r = Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(path, method: :get)
    40	
    41	      if r[:controller] == "comments" && %w[show redirect_from_short_id show_short_id].include?(r[:action])
    42	        self.to_comment = Comment.find_by(short_id: r[:id])
    43	      elsif r[:controller] == "stories" && r[:action] == "show"
    44	        # check if the url ends with a comment anchor
    45	        if (m = url.to_s.match(/#c_([0-9A-Za-z]{6,8}\z)/))
    46	          self.to_comment = Comment.find_by(short_id: m[1])
    47	        else
    48	          self.to_story = Story.find_by(short_id: r[:id])
    49	        end
    50	      end
    51	    end
    52	  rescue ActionController::RoutingError
    53	    # ignore invalid URLs from recognize_path
    54	  end
    55	
    56	  # acts idempotently, ignoring multiple Links to the same url/story/comment
    57	  # ignores validation errors; some comments have bad links ('#', 'https://foo/', 'https://http://apple.com')
    58	  def self.recreate_from_comment! c
    59	    Link.transaction do
    60	      Link.where(from_comment_id: c.id).delete_all
    61	      c.parsed_links.each(&:save)
    62	    end
    63	  end
    64	
    65	  # acts idempotently, ignoring multiple Links to the same url/story/comment
    66	  def self.recreate_from_story! s
    67	    Link.transaction do
    68	      Link.where(from_story_id: s.id).delete_all
    69	      s.parsed_links.each(&:save)
    70	    end
    71	  end
    72	
    73	  # Recalculates the cached normalized URL for each link, should be run whenever
    74	  # Utils.normalize changes so that new story submissions can locate links in old
    75	  # discussions correctly.
    76	  def self.refresh_normalized_urls!
    77	    # Note that we assume normalization doesn't affect whether a URL links to a comment or story
    78	    # on site, as calculated in url=
    79	    Link.find_each { |l| l.update_columns normalized_url: Utils.normalize(l.url) }
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  private
    83	
    84	  def valid_url
    85	    errors.add(:url, "is not valid") unless url.match?(Utils::URL_RE)
    86	  end
    87	
    88	  def validate_from_presence_xor
    89	    errors.add(:base, "from_story xor from_comment must be present") unless from_story.present? ^ from_comment.present?
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def validate_only_one_to
    93	    errors.add(:base, "Both to_story and to_comment cannot be set") if to_story.present? && to_comment.present?
    94	  end
    95	end

    55	    moderation_01j79djrajfa5a56bbpmb6msde
    56	    moderation_01j8myn90rey8axkbw0hntxfqe

    60	    moderation_01jdgt4btvfvear3hphy79104f

    63	    moderation_01jnywh2rxekqa61seqmk8z3f7
    64	  ].freeze

    71	  after_create :send_message_to_moderated
    72	  after_create -> { ModActivity.create_for! self }
    73	
    74	  def send_message_to_moderated
    75	    m = Message.new
    76	    m.author_user_id = moderator_user_id
    77	
    78	    # mark as deleted by author so they don't fill up moderator message boxes
    79	    m.deleted_by_author = true
    80	
    81	    if story
    82	      m.recipient_user_id = story.user_id
    83	      m.subject = "Your story has been edited by " +
    84	        (is_from_suggestions? ? "user suggestions" : "a moderator")
    85	      m.body = "Your story [#{story.title}](" \
    86	        "#{Routes.title_url(story)}) has been edited with the following " \
    87	        "changes:\n" \
    88	        "\n" \
    89	        "> *#{action}*\n"
    90	
    91	      if reason.present?
    92	        m.body << "\n" \
    93	          "The reason given:\n" \
    94	          "\n" \
    95	          "> *#{reason}*\n" \
    96	          "\n" \
    97	          "Maybe the guidelines on topicality are useful: https://lobste.rs/about#topicality"
    98	      end
    99	
   100	    elsif comment
   101	      m.recipient_user_id = comment.user_id
   102	      m.subject = "Your comment has been moderated"
   103	      m.body = "Your comment on [#{comment.story.title}](" \
   104	        "#{Routes.title_url comment.story}) has been moderated:\n" \
   105	        "\n" <<
   106	        comment.comment.split("\n").map { |l| "> #{l}" }.join("\n")
   107	
   108	      if reason.present?
   109	        m.body << "\n" \
   110	          "The reason given:\n" \
   111	          "\n" \
   112	          "> *#{reason}*\n"
   113	      end
   114	
   115	    else
   116	      # no point in alerting deleted users, they can't login to read it
   117	      return
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    return if m.recipient_user_id == m.author_user_id
   121	
   122	    m.body << "\n" \
   123	      "*This is an automated message.*"
   124	
   125	    m.save!
   126	  end

   131	    fks = [comment_id, domain_id, origin_id, story_id, category_id, tag_id, user_id].compact.length
   132	    errors.add(:base, "moderation should be linked to only one object") if fks != 1
   133	  end
   134	end

app/views/about/about.html.erb:6:  <div class="story_text">
app/views/about/about.html.erb:38:    Some rules of thumb for great stories to submit:
app/views/about/about.html.erb:67:    As a rule of thumb, self-promo should be less than a quarter of one's stories and comments.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:73:  When links or stories are submitted, they must be tagged by the submitter from a list of predefined <a href="/tags">tags</a>.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:77:  All users see all stories by default.

app/views/about/about.html.erb:166:  All story and comment ranking on this site comes from user activity.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:168:  Moderators have no ability to raise or <a href="http://www.righto.com/2013/11/how-hacker-news-ranking-really-works.html">lower</a> the rankings of stories or comments (besides voting like any user).
app/views/about/about.html.erb:169:  Per-tag <a href="/filters">hotness modifiers</a> affect all stories with those tags;
app/views/about/about.html.erb:172:  (look for <tt>utm_</tt> in <a href="https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/blob/main/app/models/story.rb">story.rb</a>).
app/views/about/about.html.erb:178:          Users can flag stories and comments when there is a problem that needs moderator attention.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:179:          If a story or comment is flagged at least twice, it is added to the moderation review queue.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:187:          For stories, these are:
app/views/about/about.html.erb:190:              <li><b>Off-topic</b> which should be used when stories do not meet the site's definition of <a href="#topicality">topicality</a>.</li>
app/views/about/about.html.erb:192:              <li><b>Broken Link</b> which should be used for links that present a <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status">client or server error response</a> or present a "paywall" which requires registration or payment to pass.</li>
app/views/about/about.html.erb:201:              <li><b>Off-topic</b> which should be used when a comment is drifting into meta or topics that aren't related to the story.</li>
app/views/about/about.html.erb:211:  Less than 1% of stories or comments get flagged, and users are not automatically punished by flags.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:212:  Users whose stories or comments are getting flagged significantly more often are strongly encouraged to talk to mods about what's not working.
app/views/about/about.html.erb:213:  (Please don't use flagging to try to push down topics or people you don't like; click "hide" on the story and <a href="https://xkcd.com/386">move on</a> rather than clutter up the mod dashboard with false alarms.)

   117	  def perform_comment_search!
   118	    query = Comment.accessible_to_user(searcher).for_presentation
   119

   137	      when :domain

   139	        return invalid("A story can't be from multiple domains at once") if n_domains > 1
   140	        query.joins!(story: [:domain]).where!(story: {domains: {domain: value.to_s}})
   141	      when :submitter

   143	        return invalid("A story only has one submitter") if n_submitters > 1
   144	        query.joins!(story: :user).where!(story: {users: {username: value.to_s}})
   145	      when :tag

   147	        tags ||= Tag.none.select(:id)
   148	        tags.or!(Tag.where(tag: value.to_s))
   149	      # TODO unknown tag
   150	      when :title
   151	        title = true
   152	        value = flatten_title value
   153	        query.where!(
   154	          story: Story.joins(:story_text).where(
   155	            "MATCH(story_texts.title) AGAINST ('+#{value}' in boolean mode)"
   156	          )
   157	        )
   158	      when :url
   159	        url = true
   160	        query
   161	          .joins!(:story)
   162	          .and!(
   163	            Story.where(url: value.to_s)
   164	              .or(Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(value)))

   166	      when :negated
   167	        # TODO

   185	    if tags

### app/controllers/search_controller.rb

     3	class SearchController < ApplicationController
     4	  before_action :show_title_h1
     5	  before_action :ignore_searx
     6	
     7	  def index
     8	    @title = "Search"
     9	
    10	    @search = Search.new(search_params, @user)
    11	
    12	    if !@user && params[:q].to_s.starts_with?("https://")
    13	      flash[:error] = "Sorry, you have to log in to search for a URL. We're getting hammered by a spambot with many thousands of IPs. More info at https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/issues/1814"
    14	      @results = []
    15	    else
    16	      @results = @search.results
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    if @user && @search.results
    20	      if params[:what] == "stories"
    21	        votes = Vote.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(@user.id, @search.results.map(&:id))
    22	        @search.results.each do |r|
    23	          r.current_vote = votes.try(:[], r.id)
    24	        end
    25	      end
    26	      @results = if params[:what] == "comments"
    27	        CommentVoteHydrator.new(@search.results, @user)
    28	      else
    29	        @search.results
    30	      end
    31	    end
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  private
    35	
    36	  # searx is a meta-search engine, instances send endless garbage traffic to our most-expensive
    37	  # endpoint https://github.com/searx/searx/blob/master/searx/settings.yml#L807
    38	  # If you are maintaining a searx fork, please don't 'fix' your targeting of this site.
    39	  def ignore_searx
    40	    return unless params[:utf8] == "✓"
    41	    @search = Search.new({results_count: 0}, nil)
    42	    @results = []
    43	    render :index
    44	  end
    45	
    46	  def search_params
    47	    params.permit(:q, :what, :order, :page, :authenticity_token)
    48	  end
    49	end

    21	
    22	    comment = story.comments.build

    28	      # includes parent story_id to ensure this comment's story_id matches

    31	      if !comment.parent_comment

    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    # sometimes on slow connections people resubmit; silently accept it
    37	    if (already = Comment.find_by(user: comment.user,
    38	      story: comment.story,
    39	      parent_comment_id: comment.parent_comment_id,
    40	      comment: comment.comment))
    41	      render_created_comment(already)

    48	    end
    49	
    50	    if comment.valid? && params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
    51	      comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}

    55	      comment.parent_comment&.update_column(:last_reply_at, Time.current)
    56	      NotifyCommentJob.perform_later(comment)

### app/controllers/inbox_controller.rb
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class InboxController < ApplicationController
     4	  before_action :require_logged_in_user
     5	  before_action :set_page, only: [:all]
     6	  after_action :update_read_at, only: [:all, :unread]
     7	
     8	  def index
     9	    if @user.inbox_count > 0
    10	      redirect_to inbox_unread_path
    11	    else
    12	      redirect_to inbox_all_path
    13	    end
    14	  end
    15	
    16	  def all
    17	    notifications_per_page = 25
    18	
    19	    @notifications = @user
    20	      .notifications
    21	      .offset((@page - 1) * notifications_per_page)
    22	      .limit(notifications_per_page)
    23	      .order(created_at: :desc)
    24	      .preload(user: [:hidings, :votes], notifiable: {story: [:tags, :user], user: [:comments], author: [], parent_comment: []})
    25	    apply_current_vote
    26	
    27	    @has_more = @user.notifications.count > (@page * notifications_per_page)
    28	
    29	    respond_to do |format|
    30	      format.html
    31	      format.json { render json: @notifications }
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	
    35	  def unread
    36	    @notifications = @user
    37	      .notifications
    38	      .where(read_at: nil)
    39	      .order(created_at: :desc)
    40	      .preload(user: [:hidings, :votes], notifiable: {story: [:tags, :user], user: [:comments], author: [], parent_comment: []})
    41	    apply_current_vote
    42	
    43	    respond_to do |format|
    44	      format.html { render :all }
    45	      format.json { render json: @notifications }
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  private
    50	
    51	  def update_read_at
    52	    @notifications.where(read_at: nil).touch_all(:read_at)
    53	  end
    54	
    55	  def set_page
    56	    @page = params[:page].to_i
    57	    if @page == 0
    58	      @page = 1
    59	    elsif @page < 0 || @page > (2**32)
    60	      raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def apply_current_vote
    65	    comment_notifications = @notifications.filter { |n| n.notifiable_type == "Comment" }.map(&:notifiable)
    66	    comment_notifications = CommentVoteHydrator.new(comment_notifications, @user)
    67	  end
    68	end

### app/controllers/mod/flagged_controller.rb

     4	# to be used elsewhere, remember to make them into model scopes.

     6	class Mod::FlaggedController < Mod::ModController

     9	  def flagged_stories
    10	    @title = "Flagged Stories"

    12	      .includes(:user, :tags)
    13	      .where("flags > 1")
    14	      .order("stories.id DESC"))
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  def flagged_comments
    18	    @title = "Flagged Comments"

    20	      .eager_load(:user, :hat, story: :user, votes: :user)
    21	      .where("comments.flags >= 2")
    22	      .where("(select count(*) from votes where
    23	                votes.comment_id = comments.id and
    24	                vote < 0 and
    25	                votes.reason != 'M') > 2") # Me-Too comments rarely need attention
    26	      .order("comments.id DESC"))
    27	  end

    33	    @agg = fc.aggregates
    34	    @commenters = fc.commenters
    35	  end
    36	end

    11	    end
    12

    24	    Rails.cache.delete("users_tree_#{User.last.id}") # UsersController#tree

    26	    redirect_to user_path(@reparent_user), flash: {success: "User been has reparented to you."}
    27	  end

### app/controllers/moderations_controller.rb
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class ModerationsController < ApplicationController
     4	  ENTRIES_PER_PAGE = 50
     5	
     6	  before_action :show_title_h1
     7	
     8	  def index
     9	    @title = "Moderation Log"
    10	    @moderators = ["(All)", "(Moderators)", "(Users)"] + User.moderators.map(&:username)
    11	
    12	    @moderator = moderation_params.fetch("moderator", "(All)")
    13	    @what = {
    14	      stories: moderation_params.dig(:what, :stories),
    15	      comments: moderation_params.dig(:what, :comments),
    16	      tags: moderation_params.dig(:what, :tags),
    17	      users: moderation_params.dig(:what, :users),
    18	      domains: moderation_params.dig(:what, :domains),
    19	      origins: moderation_params.dig(:what, :origins),
    20	      categories: moderation_params.dig(:what, :categories)
    21	    }
    22	    @what.transform_values! { true } if @what.values.none?
    23	
    24	    @moderations = Moderation.all.eager_load(:moderator,
    25	      :story,
    26	      {comment: [:story, :user]},
    27	      :tag,
    28	      :user,
    29	      :domain,
    30	      :origin,
    31	      :category)
    32	
    33	    # filter based on target
    34	    @moderations = case @moderator
    35	    when "(All)"
    36	      @moderations
    37	    when "(Moderators)"
    38	      @moderations.where.not(moderator_user_id: nil)
    39	    when "(Users)"
    40	      @moderations.where(is_from_suggestions: true)
    41	    else
    42	      @moderations.joins(:moderator).where(users: {username: @moderator})
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    # filter based on type of thing moderated
    46	    @what.each do |type, checked|
    47	      next if checked
    48	      @moderations = @moderations.where("#{type.to_s.singularize}_id": nil)
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    # paginate
    52	    @pages = helpers.page_count(@moderations.count, ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
    53	    @page = moderation_params.fetch(:page) { 1 }.to_i
    54	
    55	    if @page <= 0 || @page > (2**32) || (@pages > 0 && @page > @pages)
    56	      raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    @moderations = @moderations
    60	      .offset((@page - 1) * ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
    61	      .order("moderations.created_at desc")
    62	      .limit(ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
    63	  end
    64	
    65	  private
    66	
    67	  def moderation_params
    68	    @moderation_params ||= params.permit(:moderator, :page,
    69	      what: %i[stories comments tags users domains origins categories])
    70	  end
    71	end

   101	  def ban
   102	    buser = User.where(username: params[:username]).first
   103	    if !buser
   104	      flash[:error] = "Invalid user."
   105	      return redirect_to "/"
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    if params[:reason].blank?
   109	      flash[:error] = "You must give a reason for the ban."
   110	      return redirect_to user_path(user: buser.username)
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    buser.ban_by_user_for_reason!(@user, params[:reason])
   114	
   115	    flash[:success] = "User has been banned."
   116	    redirect_to user_path(user: buser.username)

   118	
   119	  def unban
   120	    buser = User.where(username: params[:username]).first
   121	    if !buser
   122	      flash[:error] = "Invalid user."
   123	      return redirect_to "/"
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    buser.unban_by_user!(@user, params[:reason])
   127	
   128	    flash[:success] = "User has been unbanned."
   129	    redirect_to user_path(user: buser.username)
   130	  end
   131	
   132	  def standing
   133	    @interval = time_interval("1m")
   134	
   135	    fc = FlaggedCommenters.new(@interval[:param], 1.day)
   136	    @fc_flagged = fc.commenters.map { |_, c| c[:n_flags] }.sort
   137	    @flagged_user_stats = fc.check_list_for(@showing_user)
   138	
   139	    rows = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query("
   140	      select
   141	        n_flags, count(*) as n_users
   142	      from (
   143	        select
   144	          comments.user_id, sum(flags) as n_flags
   145	        from
   146	          comments
   147	        where
   148	          comments.created_at >= now() - interval #{@interval[:dur]} #{@interval[:intv]}
   149	        group by comments.user_id) count_by_user
   150	      group by 1
   151	      order by 1 asc;
   152	    ").rows
   153	    users = Array.new(@fc_flagged.last.to_i + 1, 0)
   154	    rows.each { |r| users[r.first] = r.last }
   155	    @lookup = rows.to_h
   156	
   157	    @flagged_comments = @showing_user.comments
   158	      .where({comments: {flags: 1..}})
   159	      .where("comments.created_at >= ?", "now() - interval #{@interval[:dur]} #{@interval[:intv]}")
   160	      .joins(:story)
   161	      .includes(:user, :hat, story: :user)
   162	      .order(id: :desc)
   163	    @flagged_comments = CommentVoteHydrator.new(@flagged_comments, @user)
   164	  end
   165	
   166	  private
   167	
   168	  def load_showing_user
   169	    # This is for the enumerator, a bot that agressively tries to enumerate accounts via Tor + VPNs.
   170	    # At least it's obvious from its outdated user agent? So let's lie to it.
   171	    @showing_user = if request.user_agent == "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:64.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/64.0"
   172	      if rand(0..100) == 23
   173	        User.new(username: params[:username], created_at: rand(0..9999).days.ago)
   174	      end
   175	    else
   176	      User.find_by(username: params[:username])
   177	    end
   178	    # case-insensitive search by username
   179	
   180	    if @showing_user.nil?
   181	      @title = "User not found"
   182	      render action: :not_found, status: 404
   183	      return false
   184	    end
   185	
   186	    # now a case-sensitive check
   187	    if params[:username] != @showing_user.username
   188	      redirect_to username: @showing_user.username
   189	      false
   190	    end
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def only_user_or_moderator
   194	    if params[:username] == @user.username || @user.is_moderator?
   195	      true
   196	    else
   197	      redirect_to(user_path(params[:username]))
   198	    end
   199	  end
   200	end

### app/jobs/notify_comment_job.rb
     1	class NotifyCommentJob < ApplicationJob
     2	  queue_as :default
     3	
     4	  def perform(*comments)
     5	    comments.each do |comment|
     6	      deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
     7	    end
     8	  end
     9	
    10	  def deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
    11	    notified = deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
    12	    deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
    16	    mentions = comment.comment.scan(/\B[@~]([\w-]+)/).flatten.uniq
    17	    # Remove username of author and anyone already notified about the reply.
    18	    # If they have email_replies off, a reply that @mentions them will not generate a
    19	    # mention email. email_replies trumps email_mentions to minimize unwanted emails.
    20	    to_notify = mentions - [comment.user.username] - notified
    21	
    22	    # every user gets a Notification, which may be filtered out from those views so that unhiding a
    23	    # story reveals the notifications
    24	    to_notify = User.active.where(username: to_notify)
    25	    to_notify.find_each do |u|
    26	      u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    # but there's no recalling an email or pushover, so sending those has to reflect story hiding
    30	    not_hiding_users = to_notify.left_outer_joins(:hidings).where(hidden_stories: {id: nil})
    31	    not_hiding_users.find_each do |u|
    32	      if u.email_mentions?
    33	        begin
    34	          EmailReplyMailer.mention(comment, u).deliver_now
    35	        rescue => e
    36	          # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      if u.pushover_mentions?
    41	        u.pushover!(
    42	          title: "#{Rails.application.name} mention by " \
    43	            "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
    44	          message: comment.comment,
    45	          url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
    46	          url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
    47	        )
    48	      end
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	
    52	  def users_following_thread(comment)
    53	    users_following_thread = Set.new
    54	    if comment.user.id != comment.story.user.id && comment.story.user_is_following
    55	      users_following_thread << comment.story.user
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    if comment.parent_comment_id &&
    59	        (u = comment.parent_comment.try(:user)) &&
    60	        u.id != comment.user.id &&
    61	        u.is_active?
    62	      users_following_thread << u
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    users_following_thread
    66	  end
    67	
    68	  def deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
    69	    notified = []
    70	
    71	    to_notify = users_following_thread(comment)
    72	    to_notify.each do |u|
    73	      u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
    74	      notified << u.username
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    hiding_users = HiddenStory.where(story: comment.story).pluck(:user_id)
    78	    to_notify.each do |u|
    79	      next if hiding_users.include? u.id
    80	
    81	      if u.email_replies?
    82	        begin
    83	          EmailReplyMailer.reply(comment, u).deliver_now
    84	        rescue => e
    85	          # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
    86	        end
    87	      end
    88	
    89	      if u.pushover_replies?
    90	        u.pushover!(
    91	          title: "#{Rails.application.name} reply from " \
    92	            "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
    93	          message: comment.comment,
    94	          url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
    95	          url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
    96	        )
    97	      end
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    notified
   101	  end
   102	end

### app/jobs/send_webmention_job.rb
     1	class SendWebmentionJob < ApplicationJob
     2	  queue_as :default
     3	
     4	  def endpoint_from_body(html)
     5	    doc = Nokogiri::HTML(html)
     6	
     7	    if !doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").empty?
     8	      doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
     9	    elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").empty?
    10	      doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
    11	    elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").empty?
    12	      doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
    13	    end
    14	  end
    15	
    16	  def endpoint_from_headers(header)
    17	    return unless header
    18	
    19	    if (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"/))
    20	      matches[1]
    21	    elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel=webmention/))
    22	      matches[1]
    23	    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"; <([^>]+)>/))
    24	      matches[1]
    25	    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel=webmention; <([^>]+)>/))
    26	      matches[1]
    27	    elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"/))
    28	      matches[1]
    29	    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"; <([^>]+)>/))
    30	      matches[1]
    31	    end
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  # Some pages could return a relative link as their webmention endpoint.
    35	  # We need to translate this relative link to an absolute one.
    36	  def uri_to_absolute(uri, req_uri)
    37	    abs_uri = URI.parse(uri)
    38	    if abs_uri.host
    39	      # Already absolute.
    40	      uri
    41	    else
    42	      abs_uri.host = req_uri.host
    43	      abs_uri.scheme = req_uri.scheme
    44	      abs_uri.port = req_uri.port
    45	      abs_uri
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  def send_webmention(source, target, endpoint)
    50	    sp = Sponge.new
    51	    sp.timeout = 10
    52	    # Don't check SSL certificate here for backward compatibility, security risk
    53	    # is minimal.
    54	    sp.ssl_verify = false
    55	    sp.fetch(endpoint.to_s, :post, {
    56	      "source" => URI.encode_www_form_component(source),
    57	      "target" => URI.encode_www_form_component(target)
    58	    }, nil, {}, 3)
    59	  end
    60	
    61	  def perform(story)
    62	    # Could have been deleted between creation and now
    63	    return if story.is_gone?
    64	    # Need a URL to send the webmention to
    65	    return if story.url.blank?
    66	    # Don't try to send webmentions in dev
    67	    return if Rails.env.development?
    68	
    69	    sp = Sponge.new
    70	    sp.timeout = 10
    71	    begin
    72	      response = sp.fetch(URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(story.url), :get, {}, nil, {
    73	        "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} webmention endpoint lookup"
    74	      }, 3)
    75	    rescue BadIPsError, NoIPsError, DNSError, Errno::ECONNREFUSED, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, TooManyRedirects, Zlib::DataError
    76	      # other people's DNS/hosting issues (usually transient); just drop the webmention on the floor
    77	      return
    78	    end
    79	    return unless response
    80	
    81	    wm_endpoint_raw = endpoint_from_headers(response["link"]) ||
    82	      endpoint_from_body(response.body.to_s)
    83	    return unless wm_endpoint_raw
    84	
    85	    wm_endpoint = uri_to_absolute(wm_endpoint_raw, URI.parse(story.url))
    86	    send_webmention(Routes.story_short_id_url(story), story.url, wm_endpoint)
    87	  end
    88	end

### app/jobs/mastodon_sync_list_job.rb
     1	# This script is mostly correct but not running in production because:
     2	#  1. Mastodon lists aren't public, so there's no value to it maintaining a list:
     3	#     https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/issues/8208
     4	#  2. It needs more error-handling. in prod it throws errors about linked accounts that have since
     5	#     been deleted, or are on instances that are down (temporarilty or permanently, etc).
     6	#
     7	# I'm going to leave this code alone until #1 is sorted, then think about how to handle errors:
     8	# we could unlink accounts (bad for temp failures), warn in settings (good but have to figure out
     9	# where to save that state), ignore (probably ok, but a waste of resources for perm failures).
    10	
    11	class MastodonSyncListJob < ApplicationJob
    12	  queue_as :default
    13	
    14	  def perform(*args)
    15	    return unless Mastodon.enabled? && Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.list_id
    16	
    17	    # accept all follow requests
    18	    follow_requests = Mastodon.get_follow_requests
    19	    follow_requests.each do |id|
    20	      Mastodon.accept_follow_request(id)
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    # reconcile public list with our linked accounts
    24	    acct_to_id = Mastodon.get_list_accounts
    25	    their_users = acct_to_id.keys.to_set
    26	    our_users = Set.new(
    27	      User.active.where("settings LIKE '%mastodon_username:%'")
    28	        .select { |u| u.mastodon_username.present? }
    29	        .map { |u| "#{u.mastodon_username}@#{u.mastodon_instance}" }
    30	    )
    31	
    32	    # puts "their_users", their_users
    33	    # puts "our_users", our_users
    34	    to_remove = their_users - our_users
    35	    to_add = our_users - their_users
    36	
    37	    # Mastodon requires following a user to add them to a list
    38	
    39	    if to_remove.any?
    40	      Mastodon.remove_list_accounts(to_remove.map { |a| acct_to_id[a] })
    41	      to_remove.each do |a|
    42	        Mastodon.unfollow_account(acct_to_id[a])
    43	      end
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    if to_add.any?
    47	      add_ids = []
    48	      to_add.each do |a|
    49	        id = Mastodon.get_account_id(a)
    50	        add_ids << id
    51	        Mastodon.follow_account(id)
    52	      end
    53	      Mastodon.add_list_accounts(add_ids)
    54	    end
    55	  end
    56	end

### app/mailboxes/inbox_mailbox.rb
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class InboxMailbox < ApplicationMailbox
     4	  before_processing :required_info
     5	
     6	  def process
     7	    c = Comment.new(user: sending_user, comment: @decoded, is_from_email: true)
     8	    if parent.is_a?(Comment)
     9	      c.story_id = parent.story_id
    10	      c.parent_comment_id = parent.id
    11	    else
    12	      c.story_id = parent.id
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    # If we fail to parse the comment, just throw an exception.
    16	    c.save!
    17	  end
    18	
    19	  private
    20	
    21	  def forcibly_convert_to_utf8(string)
    22	    if string.encoding == Encoding::UTF_8 && string.valid_encoding?
    23	      return string
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    string.b.encode(
    27	      Encoding::UTF_8,
    28	      invalid: :replace,
    29	      undef: :replace,
    30	      replace: "?"
    31	    )
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  def parent
    35	    return @parent if @parent
    36	
    37	    # Emails In-Reply-To formatted like
    38	    # story.<short_id>.<timestamp of story creation>@<app domain>
    39	    # We throw away the timestamp, since we only care about the short ID.
    40	    irt = mail.in_reply_to.to_s.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9@.]/, "")
    41	
    42	    if (m = irt.match(/^comment\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
    43	      @parent = Comment.where(short_id: m[1]).first
    44	    elsif (m = irt.match(/^story\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
    45	      @parent = Story.where(short_id: m[1]).first
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    @parent
    49	  end
    50	
    51	  def required_info
    52	    body = if mail.content_type.blank? # old, non-multipart message
    53	      mail.decoded.to_s
    54	    elsif /text\/plain/.match?(mail.content_type.to_s)
    55	      mail.body.to_s
    56	    elsif mail.multipart?
    57	      # parts[0] - multipart/alternative
    58	      #  parts[0].parts[0] - text/plain
    59	      #  parts[0].parts[1] - text/html
    60	      if (found = mail.parts.first.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }
    61	         ).any?
    62	        found.first.body.to_s
    63	      elsif (found = mail.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }).any?
    64	        found.first.body.to_s
    65	      else
    66	        bounced!
    67	      end
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    @decoded = tidy(forcibly_convert_to_utf8(body))
    71	    if @decoded == "" || sending_user.nil? || parent.nil?
    72	      # We could email the user about the bounce, but that doesn't seem
    73	      # to align with current behaviour.
    74	      bounced!
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	
    78	  def user_token
    79	    (mail.to.find { |e| e =~ /^#{Rails.application.shortname}-/ } || "")[/-([^@]*)@/, 1]
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  def sending_user
    83	    return @sending_user if @sending_user
    84	
    85	    if (user = User.find_by(mailing_list_token: user_token)) &&
    86	        user.is_active?
    87	      @sending_user = user
    88	      user
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def tidy(body)
    93	    body.gsub(/^-- \n.+\z/m, "")                # sig
    94	      .gsub(/^(On|on|at) .*\n\n?(>.*\n)+/m, "") # top-posting
    95	      .strip
    96	  end
    97	end

     3	class EmailReplyMailer < ApplicationMailer
     4	  def reply(comment, user)
     5	    @comment = comment
     6	    @user = user
     7	
     8	    @replied_to = "you"
     9	    if @comment.parent_comment.nil?
    10	      @replied_to = "your story"
    11	    elsif @comment.parent_comment.user != @user
    12	      @replied_to = @comment.parent_comment.user.username
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    # threading
    16	    set_headers
    17	
    18	    mail(
    19	      to: user.email,
    20	      subject: "[#{Rails.application.name}] Reply from " \
    21	                  "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}"
    22	    )
    23	  end
    24	
    25	  def mention(comment, user)
    26	    @comment = comment
    27	    @user = user
    28	
    29	    set_headers
    30	
    31	    mail(
    32	      to: user.email,
    33	      subject: "[#{Rails.application.name}] Mention from " \
    34	                  "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}"
    35	    )
    36	  end
    37	
    38	  private
    39	
    40	  def set_headers
    41	    headers "Message-Id" => @comment.mailing_list_message_id,
    42	      "References" => ([@comment.story.mailing_list_message_id] + @comment.parents.map(&:mailing_list_message_id))
    43	        .map { |r| "<#{r}>" },
    44	      "In-Reply-To" => @comment.parent_comment.present? ?
    45	        @comment.parent_comment.mailing_list_message_id :
    46	        @comment.story.mailing_list_message_id
    47	  end
    48	end

   291	    respond_to do |format|
   292	      format.html { render action: "index" }
   293	      format.rss { render action: "stories", layout: false }
   294	      format.json { render json: @stories }
   295	    end
   296	  end
   297	
   298	  def top
   299	    length = time_interval(params[:length])
   300	    if length[:placeholder]
   301	      respond_to do |format|
   302	        format.html { redirect_to top_path(length: "1w") }
   303	        format.rss { redirect_to top_path(length: "1w", format: :rss) }
   304	      end
   305	      return
   306	    end
   307	
   308	    @stories, @show_more = get_from_cache(top: true, length: length) {
   309	      paginate Story.top(@user, length, filtered_tags.map(&:id))
   310	    }
   311	
   312	    @title = if length[:dur] > 1
   313	      "Top Stories of the Past #{length[:dur]} #{length[:intv].pluralize(length[:dur])}"
   314	    else
   315	      "Top Stories of the Past #{length[:intv]}"
   316	    end
   317	    @above = "stories/subnav"
   318	    @below = "home/top"
   319	
   320	    @rss_link ||= {
   321	      title: "RSS 2.0 - " + @title,
   322	      href: "/top/#{length[:param]}/rss"
   323	    }
   324	
   325	    respond_to do |format|
   326	      format.html { render action: "index" }
   327	      format.rss { render action: "stories", layout: false }
   328	    end
   329	  end
   330	
   331	  def upvoted
   332	    @stories, @show_more = get_from_cache(upvoted: true, user: @user) {
   333	      paginate @user.upvoted_stories.includes(:tags).order("votes.id DESC")
   334	    }
   335	
   336	    @title = "Upvoted Stories"
   337	    @above = "saved/subnav"
   338	
   339	    @rss_link = {
   340	      title: "RSS 2.0 - Upvoted Stories",
   341	      href: user_token_link(upvoted_stories_path(format: :rss))
   342	    }
   343	
   344	    respond_to do |format|
   345	      format.html { render action: :index }
   346	      format.rss {
   347	        if @user && params[:token].present?
   348	          @title += " - Private feed for #{@user.username}"
   349	        end

   351	        render action: "stories", layout: false
   352	      }
   353	      format.json { render json: @stories }
   354	    end

   357	  private
   358	
   359	  def page
   360	    p = params[:page].to_i
   361	    if p == 0
   362	      p = 1
   363	    elsif p < 0 || p > (2**32)
   364	      raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
   365	    end
   366	    p
   367	  end
   368	
   369	  def paginate(scope)
   370	    StoriesPaginator.new(scope, page, @user).get
   371	  end
   372	
   373	  def get_from_cache(opts = {}, &)
   374	    # don't cache if there's a user because they can have hidden stories; visitors can filter tags by cookie
   375	    if Rails.env.development? || @user || filtered_tags.any?
   376	      yield
   377	    else
   378	      key = opts.merge(page: page).sort.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v.to_param}" }.join(" ")
   379	      begin
   380	        Rails.cache.fetch("stories #{key}", expires_in: 45, &)
   381	      rescue Errno::ENOENT => e
   382	        # Rails.logger.error "error fetching stories #{key}: #{e}"
   383	        yield
   384	      end
   385	    end
   386	  end
   387	
   388	  def tags_with_description_for_rss(tags)
   389	    tags.map { |tag| "#{tag.tag} (#{tag.description})" }.join(" ")
   390	  end
   391	end

197	  def update
   198	    if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user))
   199	      return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
   200	    end
   201	
   202	    comment.comment = params[:comment]
   203	    comment.last_edited_at = Time.current
   204	    comment.hat_id = nil
   205	    comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])
   206	
   207	    if params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
   208	      votes = Vote.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(@user.id, [comment.id])
   209	      current_user_reply_parents = @user&.ids_replied_to([comment.id]) || Hash.new { false }
   210	      comment.current_vote = votes[comment.id]
   211	      comment.vote_summary = Vote.comment_vote_summaries([comment.id])[comment.id]
   212	      comment.current_reply = current_user_reply_parents.has_key? comment.id
   213	      # not calling comment.touch because the :touch on the parent_comment association will already
   214	      # touch the updated_at columns up the reply chain to the story once
   215	      comment.parent_comment&.touch(:last_reply_at)
   216	
   217	      if !request.xhr?
   218	        return redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
   219	      end

   221	      if params[:redirect_on_submit].present?
   222	        head :ok, x_location: Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
   223	      else
   224	        render_created_comment(comment, params[:show_tree_lines])
   225	      end
   226	    else
   227	      comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
   228	
   229	      preview comment
   230	    end

   397	  def find_comment
   398	    comment = Comment.where(short_id: params[:id]).first
   399	    # convenience to use PK (from external queries) without generally permitting enumeration:
   400	    comment ||= Comment.find(params[:id]) if @user&.is_admin?
   401	
   402	    if @user && comment
   403	      comment.current_vote = Vote.where(user_id: @user.id,
   404	        story_id: comment.story_id, comment_id: comment.id).first
   405	      comment.vote_summary = Vote.comment_vote_summaries([comment.id])[comment.id]
   406	      comment.current_reply = @user.ids_replied_to([comment.id])
   407	    end
   408	
   409	    comment
   410	  end

   412	  def preview(comment)
   413	    comment.previewing = true
   414	    comment.is_deleted = false # show normal preview for deleted comments
   415	
   416	    render partial: "comments/preview",
   417	      layout: false,
   418	      content_type: "text/html",
   419	      locals: {
   420	        comment: comment,
   421	        show_tree_lines: params[:show_tree_lines]
   422	      }
   423	  end
   424	
   425	  def render_created_comment(comment, show_tree_lines = true)
   426	    if request.xhr?
   427	      render partial: "comments/postedreply", layout: false,
   428	        content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment:, show_tree_lines:}
   429	    else
   430	      redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
   431	    end
   432	  end
   433	end

     6	class Mod::FlaggedController < Mod::ModController
     7	  include IntervalHelper
     8	
     9	  def flagged_stories
    10	    @title = "Flagged Stories"
    11	    @stories = period(Story.base(@user).unmerged
    12	      .includes(:user, :tags)
    13	      .where("flags > 1")
    14	      .order("stories.id DESC"))
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  def flagged_comments
    18	    @title = "Flagged Comments"
    19	    @comments = period(Comment
    20	      .eager_load(:user, :hat, story: :user, votes: :user)
    21	      .where("comments.flags >= 2")
    22	      .where("(select count(*) from votes where
    23	                votes.comment_id = comments.id and
    24	                vote < 0 and
    25	                votes.reason != 'M') > 2") # Me-Too comments rarely need attention
    26	      .order("comments.id DESC"))
    27	  end
    28	
    29	  def commenters
    30	    @title = "Flagged Commenters"
    31	    fc = FlaggedCommenters.new(params[:period])
    32	    @interval = fc.interval
    33	    @agg = fc.aggregates
    34	    @commenters = fc.commenters
    35	  end
    36	end

1	class SuggestionsController < ApplicationController
     2	  include StoryFinder
     3	
     4	  before_action :find_story!, only: [:new, :create]
     5	  before_action :require_logged_in_user, only: [:new]
     6	  before_action :show_title_h1, only: [:new]
     7	
     8	  def create
     9	    if !@story.can_have_suggestions_from_user?(@user)
    10	      flash[:error] = "You are not allowed to offer suggestions on that story."
    11	      return redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    story_user = @story.user
    15	    inappropriate_tags = Tag
    16	      .where(tag: params[:story][:tags])
    17	      .reject { |t| t.can_be_applied_by?(story_user) }
    18	    if inappropriate_tags.length > 0
    19	      tag_error = ""
    20	      inappropriate_tags.each do |t|
    21	        tag_error += if t.privileged?
    22	          "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} as they are not a " \
    23	            "moderator, so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
    24	        elsif !t.permit_by_new_users?
    25	          "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} due to being a new " \
    26	            "user, so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
    27	        elsif !t.active?
    28	          "Cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} because it's inactive"
    29	        else
    30	          "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} " \
    31	            "so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
    32	        end
    33	      end
    34	      tag_error += ""
    35	      flash[:error] = tag_error
    36	    end
    37	
    38	    ostory = @story.dup
    39	
    40	    @story.title = params[:story][:title]
    41	    if @story.valid?
    42	      dsug = false
    43	      if @story.title != ostory.title
    44	        @story.save_suggested_title_for_user!(@story.title, @user)
    45	        dsug = true
    46	      end
    47	
    48	      sugtags = Tag
    49	        .where(tag: params[:story][:tags])
    50	        .reject { |t| !t.can_be_applied_by?(story_user) }
    51	        .map { |s| s.tag }
    52	      if @story.tags.map(&:tag).sort != sugtags.sort
    53	        @story.save_suggested_tags_for_user!(sugtags, @user)
    54	        dsug = true
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      if dsug
    58	        ostory = @story.reload
    59	        flash[:success] = "Your suggested changes have been noted."
    60	      end
    61	      redirect_to Routes.title_path ostory
    62	    else
    63	      render action: "suggest"
    64	    end
    65	  end
    66	
    67	  def new
    68	    @title = "Suggest Story Changes"
    69	    if !@story.can_have_suggestions_from_user?(@user)
    70	      flash[:error] = "You are not allowed to offer suggestions on that story."
    71	      return redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
    72	    end
    73	
    74	    if (suggested_tags = @story.suggested_taggings.where(user_id: @user.id)).any?
    75	      @story.tags = suggested_tags.map(&:tag)
    76	    end
    77	    if (tt = @story.suggested_titles.where(user_id: @user.id).first)
    78	      @story.title = tt.title
    79	    end
    80	  end
    81	end
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class StoryUrlsController < ApplicationController
     4	  def all
     5	    url = params.require(:url)
     6	
     7	    respond_to do |format|
     8	      format.json {
     9	        render json: Story.find_similar_by_url(url).for_presentation
    10	      }
    11	    end
    12	  end
    13	
    14	  def latest
    15	    url = params.require(:url)
    16	
    17	    similar_stories = Story.find_similar_by_url(url)
    18	    if similar_stories.any?
    19	      redirect_to Routes.title_path similar_stories.first
    20	    elsif @user
    21	      redirect_to new_story_path, url: url
    22	    else
    23	      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    24	    end
    25	  end
    26	end
     1	# typed: false
     2	
     3	class StoryImageController < ApplicationController
     4	  def show
     5	    short_id = params[:short_id]
     6	    story = Story.where(short_id: short_id).first!
     7	    image = StoryImage.new(story)
     8	
     9	    if image.exists?
    10	      response.headers["Expires"] = 1.hour.from_now.httpdate
    11	      send_file image.path, type: "image/png", disposition: "inline"
    12	    else
    13	      lobsters_logo = Rails.public_path.join("touch-icon-144.png")
    14	      if File.exist?(lobsters_logo)
    15	        response.headers["Expires"] = 1.hour.from_now.httpdate
    16	        send_file lobsters_logo, type: "image/png", disposition: "inline"
    17	      else
    18	        head :not_found
    19	      end
    20	    end
    21	  end
    22	end
